summary: Implemented the Stage 7E non-destructive cleanup and orphan planning layer for ignored runtime paths with workspace containment guards and active-run preservation.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_worktree_cleanup.py -q -p no:cacheprovider

tests_run.summary: 289 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

Cleanup planning validates every target.evidence: plan_cleanup_targets calls validate_cleanup_target for every candidate before classifying runtime paths; focused tests cover workspace root and outside-path rejection.

Orphan detection classifies ignored runt.evidence: Cleanup entries classify worktrees, runs, artifacts, and logs roots, extract worker_run_id from the second path segment, and skip entries whose worker_run_id is active_worker_run_ids.

Cleanup plans are structured and non-des.evidence: CleanupPlanEntry exposes requested_path, repo_relative_path, resolved_path, runtime_kind, worker_run_id, reason, operation, selected, and skip_reason; tests assert candidate directories still exist after planning.

Focused tests cover safe cleanup candida.evidence: tests/test_worktree_cleanup.py covers safe orphan runtime directories, root/outside rejection, active run skipping, unsupported root skipping, and missing worker_run_id skipping.

risks: Stage 7E does not execute deletion; future live cleanup must consume CleanupPlan.selected_entries and keep containment checks at the boundary.
The planner classifies runtime paths by convention and does not consult planning SQLite directly; a later integration slice should feed active worker-run IDs from typed planning helpers.
Orphan discovery is caller-supplied in this slice; filesystem scanning commands remain intentionally out of scope.
follow_up_tasks: Shape the next Stage 7 slice to feed active worker-run IDs from planning SQLite into cleanup planning.
Add a dry-run CLI command for cleanup/orphan plans before any live deletion command is considered.
handoff_notes: Stage 7E adds worktree_cleanup.py for non-destructive cleanup planning. Future deletion-capable commands should accept only validated CleanupPlan entries and keep live deletion behind an explicit dry-run/review gate.
